titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Helen Haywood archive

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday February 2, 2026 at 18:00

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises material related to the work of Helen Haywood, illustrator of children's books. The material includes drawings, mock-ups, and typescript drafts.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Archives Department.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Acquired with funds from the Bequest of Daniel S. Kalk

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Helen Haywood archive, Yale Center for British Art, Archives, Acquired with funds from the Bequest of Daniel S. Kalk

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is unprocessed and may not be available for immediate access.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: Series I. "Colour-Cuts" cut out minis, 1952 - 1967; Series II. Cover art and dummy books, 1952-1953; III. R. Tyndall Mini Books, 1946-1966, undated.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Helen Haywood (1907 December 31-1995 February 21) was a prolific illustrator of mid-20th century children's books including the Peter Tiggywig series. Born in England, Haywood spent her childhood in Chile, where her father was an engineer. Her grandfather was bookbinder Robert Riviere, founder of Robert Riviere and Son, and extended family included other artists and illustrators. Haywood remained in Chile with her parents and brother until 1922, when a large earthquake shook the country.

While Helen Haywood had begun making art and creating books in 1911 at the age of four after being gifted a paint set, she began her formal training at Bournemouth School of Art, Poole Hill. In 1926 she published her first book, The Mouse That Ran Away . This would mark the first of over two hundred books she would create in her lifetime.

During the late 1920s and into the 1930s, Haywood painted both miniatures and book fore-edges at Robert Riviere and Son, her grandfather's book bindery. It is unclear what else Haywood was doing at the time, or how she began to break into publishing in the late 1930s.

Early in her career, Haywood collaborated with author Isobel St. Vincent, creating illustrations for works such as Wanda the Panda and All A-Growing: the adventures of two young gardeners . While Haywood would continue to create some work with other authors, she wrote most of her own books as well. She took on commissions for greeting cards, some logos, and publications for magazines – mostly those intended for children.

Haywood's art was animal focused. While many of her works feature animal adventures, they were noted at the time for their accuracy in illustration. This went doubly for works of non-fiction intended for children, such as her dinosaur book which received accolades from the Royal Academy of Sciences.
Helen Haywood's career slowed as she grew older. She died in Bournemouth, England, on 1995 February 21.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series primarily relates to The Teddybear Book and shows various states of progress of illustration for the volume. It is unclear if this book was ever published.
The note on the original folder reads "mini books, counting books." An additional sheet of illustrations from 1967 has been interfiled with the other material and retained.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically with the original folder placed first.

did.unittitle: Bee on a flower sketch and watercolor drawing
The Book of the Sparrow cover roughs
Color rough for end papers The Teddybear Book
Rabbits in a hutch sketch and watercolor drawing
Sketches and watercolors of possible book covers
Six color plates for the Teddybear Book
Six color plates for Teddy Bear Book, color roughs
Series II. Cover art and dummy books

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arrangement of the cover art reflects the illustration process for Haywood, beinging with pencils, proceeding to painting drafts on paper, tracing paper, and then cover paintings. Material is arranged alphabetically within each grouping. Dummy books are arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series comprises cover art for various titles by Haywood and dummy books. The covers show various states of Haywood's process and include color tests, paintings on board, tracing paper that capture the figure and lettering for each cover, and cover proposals. There are several title changes in this process and no publisher is identified for the books. Due to the title changes, it is unclear if any of them moved forward at a publisher.

The dummy books are to-scale versions of the intended book with typewritten text pasted onto the pages. Rough pencil drawings provide layout information. 'Silly' Duckling was published as The 'Silly' Duckling , whereas the The Hungry Squirrel 's title remained the same. Both were published in 1967 through Litor.

did.unittitle: Pencil drawings of Postman Bear and Fancy Mouse with additional title lettering tests
Pencil drawing of The Sailor Cat with additional title lettering tests
The Sailor Cat cover with additional title lettering tests
The Circus Dog cover on tracing paper
The Fancy Mouse and The Postman Bear covers on tracing paper
The Sailor Cat cover on tracing paper
Cover designs for The Wishing Fairy and A Happy Lamb
Cover suggestions, The Circus Frog and [missing work]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A painting has been detatched from the board prior to acquisition by the Yale Center for British Art.

did.unittitle: Cover suggestions, The Postman Bear , The Fancy Mouse
George's Cake cover and text outline, "Elfin" series
Three Little Bumblebees , Hustle-Bustle Hedgehog mini books cover roughs
Pencil drawing of chicks looking at a bug
Pencil drawing of two kittens and a turtle on a see-saw
Series III. R. Tyndall Mini Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series was originally housed in a single folder, with the folder entitled mininbooks roughs, drawings, and F/A/N placed into the folder's clip and all other material interfiled before and after these organized pages.

The titles within this series were published as books for small children with the publisher R. Tyndall. Titles changed frequently between the drafts represented in this series and the final product. Jolly Snow Sweeps , is the same as The Snow Sweeps as represented in this series. Some books, such as Monito's Hat appear not to have been published. Other titles, such as Peter's Insect Friends (Hutchinson's Books for Young People : London, 1947) were published significantly earlier with a different publisher, but have been left in this series to reflect the original order of the folder itself. Several of the pencil studies likewise appear to be related to earlier titles such as Monte Mongoose (Hutchinson & Co Ltd [1950], London, 1950) and Corny 'Coon (Hutchinson & Co Ltd 1948], London, 1948).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The original folder that housed the material has been placed first in the series, followed by the pages that were held by the folder's clips. Subsequent material is arranged alphabetically. Physical arrangement in the box reflects the original order of the material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pencil sketches, layouts, lettering, drafts and notes in their original order for: The Snow Sweeps, Three Little Bumblebees, Hustle-Bustle Hedgehog, The Happy Lamb, The Silly Duckling, The Wishing Fairy.

did.unittitle: Chapter illustration drafts for unidentified book
Chapter illustration drafts for unidentified book
Cover drawings on a grid for mini books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes covers for Three Little Bumblebees, Hustle-Bustle Hedgehog, The Bongo Babe, The Sleepy Babysitter, The Zebra's Child

did.unittitle: Lettering for a book about kittens
Lettering for Tiggy Tiger, Buzz Bear, Wah Panda, Reny Fox
Monito's Hat cover drawing at scale
Monito's Hat , pencil study, marmot or prairie dog
Monty Mongoose chapter illustrations in rough book form

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Later published as Monte Mongoose , includes chapter illustrations and pencil studies for animals.

did.unittitle: Pencil rough, Squirrel and Bryony on tracing paper
Pencil rough, two kittens and a turtle on tracing paper
Pencil sudy, Grandma Raccoon, Pete Marmot
"What is it?" [pencil drawing of chicks looking at a bug on tracing paper]
Woodland Animals watercolor drawing and pencil draft pages
